research_question: Does the classic four-session US turn-of-the-month window retain positive, concentrated market returns and improve a USD 40000 whole-share SPY account after fixed costs?
null_hypothesis: The frozen turn-of-the-month account does not pass every validation, holdout, concentration, risk, stress-cost and input-identity gate.

input_identity.pre_2018_session_basis: unique strictly ordered SPY trade dates define retrospective source sessions only and cannot support strict-PIT or canonical claims

classic_window.T: last accepted SPY session of the prior calendar month
held_total_return_sessions: T+1 first accepted session of new month
T+2 second accepted session of new month
T+3 third accepted session of new month
classic_window.entry_decision: calendar-known instruction submitted before the T-1 market-on-close cutoff
classic_window.return_identity: adj_close ratio from T-1 through each held session
classic_window.episode_id: YYYY-MM of T+1, the new calendar month
classic_window.role_generation: within each adjacent pair of calendar months, T is the final source session of the old month, T-1 is its immediately prior source session, and T+1 through T+3 are the first three source sessions of the new month
classic_window.episode_complete_rule: T-1 through T+3 must be unique, strictly ordered and within one split
classic_window.incomplete_or_cross_split_episode: purge every role and every daily return belonging to the episode from account and TOM/non-TOM inference; do not relabel a purged TOM return as non-TOM

known_purges.development: episode_id 2010-01 crosses the development boundary and is purged
known_purges.validation: episode_id 2018-01 crosses the validation boundary and is purged
known_purges.retrospective_holdout: incomplete episode_id 2026-07 has T on 2026-06-30 and is censored by the cutoff; that T return is excluded from both labels

whole_share_accounting.exit_cash: entry_cash + marked_position_at_exit * (1 - one_way_cost_rate); exit cost is deliberately charged on the adjusted total-return proxy value
whole_share_accounting.zero_share_rule: zero affordable shares is an input-mechanics failure
whole_share_accounting.daily_nav_timeline: start each split with initial capital as the prior peak; on T-1 record post-entry-cost NAV at close with no exposed return day; on T through T+2 record cash plus adjusted marked position; on T+3 record post-exit-cost cash; all other sessions record cash
whole_share_accounting.B1_timeline: enter at the first split session raw close, record post-entry-cost NAV on that session, hold from the next session through the final split session, and exit at the final split session adjusted proxy value with the same cost formula
whole_share_accounting.complete_day_count: number of accepted sessions in the split, including the first post-entry-cost NAV observation and final post-exit-cost observation
whole_share_accounting.corporate_action_proxy: adjusted-close total-return ratio; not account-level strict PIT evidence

splits.combined: concatenate separately evaluated validation and holdout daily net-return series; each split starts with its own USD 40000 and bears its own initial/final account costs

comparators.B1_SPY_BUY_HOLD: whole-share SPY from first split close through final split close with identical costs
comparators.B2_SPY_CLASSIC_TURN_OF_MONTH: whole-share classic four-session window and cash otherwise

metrics.annualized_volatility: sample standard deviation of daily net returns times sqrt 252
metrics.daily_maximum_drawdown: min_t(W_t / max(initial_wealth, W_s for s <= t) - 1), including entry-cost and exit-cost NAV observations
metrics.calmar: CAGR / abs(daily_maximum_drawdown); if drawdown is zero and CAGR is positive report null positive-infinity semantics, otherwise null
metrics.time_in_market: held-return sessions T through T+3 divided by all accepted split sessions; T-1 entry-close is not exposed
metrics.one_way_turnover: sum of each executed raw buy or adjusted-proxy sale notional divided by account NAV immediately before that trade
metrics.cost_drag: sum of explicit entry and exit transaction costs in USD divided by initial capital; no zero-cost rerun and no share-count change
metrics.episode_return: account cash immediately after T+3 exit divided by account NAV immediately before T-1 entry minus one
metrics.whole_share_cash_utilization: mean across episodes of executed raw entry notional divided by account NAV immediately before entry
metrics.mean_tom_daily_return: mean r_d where r_d = adj_close[d] / adj_close[previous accepted session] - 1, both endpoints are inside the split, and destination d is T, T+1, T+2 or T+3 of a retained episode
metrics.mean_non_tom_daily_return: mean of every other valid in-split r_d after excluding all returns assigned to purged or incomplete episodes
metrics.contribution_denominator: sum of terminal net dollar profits from the separately initialized validation and holdout B2 accounts at 15 bps; a nonpositive denominator fails both concentration tests
metrics.contribution_numerator: maximum of zero and the largest episode or exit-calendar-year net dollar profit; each episode is assigned to the calendar year of T+3; validation and holdout dollar profits are summed without rescaling
metrics.contribution_comparison: strictly less than 20 percent for episode and 40 percent for calendar year; equality fails

inference.statistic: mean TOM adjusted-close daily return minus mean non-TOM adjusted-close daily return
inference.block_id: YYYY-MM of each daily-return destination session
inference.resampling_unit: ordered eligible calendar-month blocks preserving every in-split daily return and label within the sampled destination month
inference.algorithm: for N ordered blocks draw exactly N block indices with replacement for each replicate using numpy Generator(PCG64(seed)); concatenate blocks in sampled order; recompute pooled TOM mean minus pooled non-TOM mean; a replicate missing either label is invalid and blocks the result

input_qualification.required_before_gates: strict definition JSON, exact code/data/snapshot/database/calendar identities, no duplicate/missing/nonfinite/nonpositive prices, exact episode counts and role order, no post-cutoff row, no database mutation or WAL

primary_gates: validation mean TOM daily return > mean non-TOM daily return
holdout mean TOM daily return > mean non-TOM daily return
combined calendar-month bootstrap 95 percent lower bound of TOM minus non-TOM mean daily return > 0
holdout B2 daily maximum drawdown is less severe than B1 and B2 Calmar > B1
15 bps combined B2 CAGR > 0 and largest year contribution < 40 percent and largest episode contribution < 20 percent
zero session price identity snapshot database or result failures and whole-share validation and holdout CAGR > 0
stop_rule: After input qualification passes, any failed gate produces RETROSPECTIVE_REPLICATION_FAIL. Input qualification failure produces INPUT_BLOCKED without evaluating gates. Do not move the window, add season/weekday/volatility/trend filters, lower costs or run another classic-window variant.
